title: Gulab Jamun Rose Syrup

seo.title: Gulab Jamun Rose Syrup: Easy Indian Dessert Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Indulge in our exquisite Gulab Jamun Rose Syrup recipe! Learn how to make this authentic Indian dessert with a fragrant rose twist at home. Perfect for festivals!

seo.h1: Gulab Jamun Rose Syrup: Authentic Indian Dessert Recipe
h2Headings: What is Gulab Jamun Rose Syrup?

seo.imageAlt: Delicious Gulab Jamun Rose Syrup served in a bowl, garnished with rose petals and pistachios.
seo.imageCaption: A close-up of our homemade Gulab Jamun Rose Syrup, glistening with fragrant rose-infused syrup.

description: Gulab Jamun Rose Syrup is a timeless Indian dessert, featuring soft, spongy milk solids fried to a golden hue and steeped in a fragrant, sweet rose-infused sugar syrup. Each bite offers a burst of floral aroma and rich sweetness, making it a perfect treat for celebrations or a comforting indulgence. This delightful confection is a staple in Indian households, cherished for its irresistible taste and melt-in-your-mouth texture.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gulab Jamun Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine milk powder, all-purpose flour, baking soda, and cardamom powder. Add the melted ghee and mix until crumbly. Gradually add milk, a tablespoon at a time, and gently knead to form a soft, smooth dough. Avoid over-kneading. Cover the dough and let it rest for 10 minutes.

instructions.title: Shape the Gulab Jamuns
instructions.text: Divide the dough into small, equal portions. Roll each portion gently between your palms to form smooth, crack-free balls. Ensure there are no cracks, as these can cause the jamuns to break during frying. Aim for approximately 15-18 small jamuns.

instructions.title: Prepare the Rose Sugar Syrup
instructions.text: While the dough rests, combine sugar and water in a wide, heavy-bottomed pan. Bring to a boil over medium heat, stirring until the sugar dissolves completely. Add the crushed green cardamom pods. Reduce heat and simmer for 5-7 minutes until the syrup becomes slightly sticky, but not a thick one-string consistency. Remove from heat and stir in the rose water. Keep the syrup warm.

instructions.title: Fry the Gulab Jamuns
instructions.text: Heat oil or ghee in a deep frying pan over very low heat. To test the temperature, drop a tiny piece of dough into the oil; it should rise slowly to the surface without browning quickly. Carefully slide the shaped jamun balls into the warm oil. Do not overcrowd the pan. Fry on low heat, gently stirring occasionally, until they turn an even golden brown color. This slow frying ensures they cook through evenly.

instructions.title: Soak the Gulab Jamuns in Syrup
instructions.text: Once the jamuns are golden brown, carefully remove them from the oil using a slotted spoon and immediately transfer them into the warm rose sugar syrup. Ensure the jamuns are fully submerged. Repeat the frying process with the remaining jamun balls.

instructions.title: Allow to Soak and Serve
instructions.text: Let the gulab jamuns soak in the rose syrup for at least 30 minutes, or preferably for 2-3 hours, to allow them to fully absorb the syrup and become soft and spongy. Serve warm or chilled, garnished with chopped pistachios or dried rose petals if desired. This dessert is best enjoyed fresh.

tips: Always fry Gulab Jamuns on very low heat. This allows them to cook thoroughly from the inside and develop a beautiful, even golden-brown crust without burning.
For perfectly smooth jamuns, ensure your dough is soft and crack-free. If the dough is too stiff, add a tiny bit more milk. If too sticky, add a sprinkle of milk powder.
The sugar syrup should be warm, not hot or cold, when you add the fried jamuns. A warm syrup helps the jamuns absorb the sweetness more effectively and prevents them from becoming hard.
article: Gulab Jamun Rose Syrup is a beloved Indian Desserts dish enjoyed across Indian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
